content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Accepted three amendments that fix real parsing/validation defects: the cascading error bug in validate_canonical_prompt_shape, missing fence tracking in split_bead_description_scope, and the markdown sub-heading leak that causes non-goals to be reclassified as must-do scope. Rejected two amendments where the behavior is intentional by design: the opt-in contract marker for prompt overrides (an escape hatch for custom prompts), and the permissive handling of canonical-looking headings after the AGENTS section (needed to support embedded repo guidance verbatim).

## Votes

- `fr-5-7eb75cd0`: Accept
  Verified in the code at lines 160-173: when found_index > expected_index (a later section appears while an earlier one is missing), expected_index never advances and seen_positions[found_index] is never set. This means every subsequent section triggers a spurious 'unexpected canonical heading' error, and the missing-section loop at lines 175-179 also reports those sections as missing. The result is (N-remaining × 2) misleading errors from a single missing section. The proposed fix — advancing expected_index past skipped sections and recording the found section — preserves the rejection (the prompt still fails) while producing accurate diagnostics. This is clearly a correctness bug in error reporting.
- `fr-5-d580dd6c`: Accept
  The split_bead_description_scope function at lines 563-583 has no fence tracking whatsoever. It calls section_kind_for_bead_description_line on every line unconditionally, so a 'Non-goals:' label or '## Non-goals' heading inside a fenced code block would be misinterpreted as a real section boundary. This would corrupt the scope classification, turning code examples into actual non-goal items or causing must-do content to be misattributed. The validator (validate_canonical_prompt_shape) already has fence tracking via is_fence_delimiter, so the pattern is established. Adding the same fence-skipping logic to split_bead_description_scope is the correct fix.
- `fr-5-dcf32291`: Reject
  The code at lines 384-394 intentionally gates validation on prompt_uses_contract(): only prompts that declare the canonical contract marker are validated against contract requirements. A --prompt-file override without the marker is treated as a generic custom prompt. This is a deliberate escape hatch — downstream stages also gate their contract-specific guidance on the same check, so the behavior is consistent throughout. Forcing all overrides through contract validation would remove a supported workflow for users who want full control over their prompt content. This is a design choice, not a defect.
- `fr-5-455d99e1`: Reject
  Once all 8 canonical sections are found (expected_index == len), both the matching branch (line 160: expected_index < len is false) and the error branch (line 166: .get(expected_index) returns None) become no-ops, so subsequent canonical-looking headings are silently ignored. The test at line 279-285 explicitly validates this: a '## Review Policy' heading after '## AGENTS / Repo Guidance' is allowed because AGENTS guidance content may legitimately contain embedded canonical heading names from repo documentation. Rejecting all post-AGENTS canonical headings would break verbatim AGENTS guidance preservation. The validator checks outer structural shape, not uniqueness of heading text within the AGENTS body. This is an intentional design tradeoff.
- `fr-5-0405b9a2`: Accept
  Verified at lines 574-576: when active_section is Some (e.g., NonGoals), any line matching markdown_heading_title (≥2 # characters) clears active_section to None. This means a subsection heading like '### Examples' inside a Non-goals block causes all subsequent bullets to fall back into must_do_lines. Concretely: 'Non-goals:\n### Examples\n- do not change CLI' would classify '- do not change CLI' as must-do scope instead of a non-goal, silently changing task semantics. The section_kind_for_bead_description_line check at line 569 doesn't help because 'Examples' doesn't match any recognized section kind. The fix should restrict mode exits to actual canonical section boundaries (recognized by section_kind_for_bead_description_line returning Some), not arbitrary markdown headings. The existing tests only cover plain-text subsection labels, leaving this markdown-heading case untested.
